file_insights.content: module configuration with dependencies, provides, tier information

patterns_discovered.description: Each stub module follows consistent structure: {module}.types.ts, index.ts, module.json
Detailed TypeScript interfaces and enums defining complete module contracts before implementation
patterns_discovered.example: AI module with 4 enums and 13+ interfaces covering content generation, image analysis, translation, suggestions, forecasting

patterns_discovered.description: Stub implementations throw AppException with 501 NOT_IMPLEMENTED status for all methods
patterns_discovered.purpose: Fail-fast approach to prevent accidental usage of unimplemented features

patterns_discovered.description: Each module includes module.json with schema validation, dependencies, provided services, events, hooks, and tier information
patterns_discovered.standardization: JSON Schema reference, semantic versioning, tier-based feature access

patterns_discovered.description: Stub modules define language enums (TR, EN, DE, FR, AR, RU) in type definitions
Each module defines event payload types for integration: created, updated, completed, dismissed events
Module metadata includes fine-grained permissions for different operations
Module.json defines feature availability by subscription tier (professional, business, enterprise)
patterns_discovered.scalability: Enables monetization and feature rollout strategy

gotchas_discovered.description: Git diff output truncated mid-module.json file showing '... (truncated, 105960 chars total)'
gotchas_discovered.impact: Cannot verify complete module.json structure for all stub modules

gotchas_discovered.description: All stub modules are type-only with no actual service implementations, only error-throwing stubs
gotchas_discovered.impact: Any attempt to use stub features will fail with 501 NOT_IMPLEMENTED

gotchas_discovered.description: Module metadata declares dependencies on 'auth', 'organization', 'menu' which may have their own stub status
Language support hardcoded to specific enum values rather than dynamic configuration
gotchas_discovered.impact: Adding new languages requires type definition changes

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created 6 stub modules (AI, Analytics, Campaign, Loyalty, Payment, Inventory) with comprehensive type definitions and module metadata
approach_outcome.total_types_defined: ~70 interfaces and enums across all modules

recommendations.recommendation: Document the stub module contract specification for developers implementing actual modules
recommendations.rationale: Ensures consistency when moving from stubs to real implementations

recommendations.recommendation: Create tests verifying stub modules throw correct AppException errors with 501 status
recommendations.rationale: Prevents accidental stub usage in production

recommendations.recommendation: Plan migration path from stub modules to real implementations with backward compatibility strategy
recommendations.rationale: Defines how consumers should transition from error-throwing stubs to working implementations

recommendations.recommendation: Validate all module.json files match JSON Schema reference and contain required fields
recommendations.rationale: Ensures module metadata consistency and proper tooling support

recommendations.recommendation: Consider feature flag system for graceful module availability detection
recommendations.rationale: Enables feature rollout without requiring explicit error handling throughout codebase

recommendations.recommendation: Evaluate making language codes dynamic or database-driven instead of hardcoded enums
recommendations.rationale: Improves scalability for future language additions

recommendations.recommendation: Add module loading metrics and initialization tracking for performance monitoring
recommendations.rationale: Helps identify slow or problematic module implementations
